value.body:
Found during a direct Node API audit of runtime source (`packages/**/src`, excluding tests, scripts, vendor, and generated protocol files).

Problem:
The Git object store directly imports `node:zlib` for loose and packed object compression. Unlike filesystem/path/crypto, this may not have a direct Effect platform service replacement, so the action is to decide and codify the boundary rather than leave raw Node imports in core Git object code.

Evidence:
- `packages/git/src/object-store/GitPackObject.ts:1` imports `inflateSync` from `node:zlib`.
- `packages/git/src/object-store/GitFilesystemObject.ts:1` imports `deflateSync` and `inflateSync` from `node:zlib`.
- `packages/git/src/object-store/GitFilesystemObject.ts:77-79` inflates loose object bytes in the read path.
- `packages/git/src/object-store/GitFilesystemObject.ts:119-124` computes object bytes/id before writing compressed objects.

Suggested remediation:
- Introduce a small compression/decompression adapter on the Git filesystem runtime, or centralize `node:zlib` in a Node-specific layer file.
- Keep object-store parsing/composition pure over `Uint8Array` and inject compression where needed.
- If direct `node:zlib` is intentionally acceptable, document it as an explicit exception to the platform-service rule.

Acceptance criteria:
- Core Git object-store modules no longer import `node:zlib` directly, or the repo has a documented exception explaining why this API remains Node-specific.
- Existing packed/loose object read/write behavior and tests remain unchanged.

frontmatter.title: Encapsulate Git object zlib operations behind an Effect/adapter boundary

value.title: Encapsulate Git object zlib operations behind an Effect/adapter boundary
